notes: v1.14.125 - UI sweep RULE B (Back and Cancel are never twins). The New-Deployment scope picker had a '‹ Back' pill AND a CANCEL button both calling nav_back() (twins), plus the per-page grid-back #work-back - three ways to abandon the same sub-flow. Fix: CANCEL is the single exit for this discard-selection sub-flow. Dropped the '‹ Back' pill from mscope_render's header (kept the 'NEW DEPLOYMENT · FROM MASTER' title); and the whole mscope sub-flow now hides #work-back via body.ops-detail (set in mscope_render; cleared on return to the deploy list/Command Center via deploy_showList/deploy_showCommandCenter or a mode switch via showMode). Browser-verified all exit paths at 412x915: picker = CANCEL only (no back pill, no #work-back); CANCEL->Command Center restores #work-back; STAGE->staged view stays in sub-flow (hidden); CREATE->deployment detail = single inline step-back; deploy LIST restores #work-back; zero console errors, no stuck state. JS + markup, redesign-only (mscope/#work-back are New-UI) -> ?legacy=1 byte-identical. node --check x3 + sw.js, brace gate, three-stamp lockstep. DEVICE-VERIFY: in the New-Deployment scope picker the only way out is CANCEL (discard) or STAGE (commit) - no duplicate back; ?legacy=1 unchanged.
